# Secret Variable Log Masking with Windmill (Python)

## Background

Windmill encrypts variables marked as secrets and automatically masks them in job logs so that sensitive values never land in `job_logs` even if a script intentionally prints them. The mask is documented in the https://www.windmill.dev/changelog/secret-masking-job-logs: the first 3 characters of the secret are kept and the rest is replaced by `*****`. Only values of 8 characters or more are masked.

Your job is to demonstrate this behaviour end-to-end against the **cloud-hosted** Windmill instance at `REDACTED`. You will declaratively define a secret Windmill Variable, write a Python script that fetches and prints the secret value through the `wmill` Python SDK, deploy both assets to the cloud workspace, execute the script, and persist the resulting job UUID so the verifier can pull its logs through the Windmill API.

## Requirements

* Authenticate the `wmill` CLI against the cloud Windmill instance using the API token, workspace ID, and base URL provided in environment variables.
* Bootstrap a local Windmill workspace under `/home/user/myproject` containing a `wmill.yaml` file and the asset folders described below.
* Read `run-id` from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable, sanitize it for Windmill identifiers (replace every `-` with `_`) and reuse the sanitized id as a unique suffix on every Windmill asset path created by this task. Refer to the sanitized id below as `${run_id_safe}`.
* Declaratively create an **encrypted** Windmill Variable at the path `f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/api_key` whose value is literally `secret_value_${run_id_safe}_padding` (this string is longer than 8 characters so Windmill will mask it). The variable must be defined via a `.variable.yaml` manifest on disk (not only via the imperative `wmill variable add` command).
* Declaratively create a Python script at `f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/print_secret` consisting of a `.py` file paired with a `.script.yaml` metadata file. The script must call `wmill.get_variable("f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/api_key")` and print the returned value to stdout (e.g., via `print(...)`).
* Push the variable, folder, and script to the cloud workspace using the declarative `wmill sync push` flow (you must pass `--include-secrets` so the secret variable is uploaded; otherwise the default settings skip secrets).
* Trigger one execution of the deployed script through the Windmill API or the `wmill` CLI, capture the resulting job UUID, and write the line `Job ID: <job_uuid>` to `/home/user/myproject/output.log` (a single line is enough).
* Do **not** print, log, or otherwise persist the plaintext secret value anywhere outside of the Windmill job's masked logs. The point of the task is to prove that Windmill masks it for you.

## Implementation Hints

* Install the CLI with `npm install -g windmill-cli` (Node.js ≥ 20 is already in the image).
* Use `wmill workspace add` (supplying `--remote`, the workspace id, and the token) followed by `wmill init` to bootstrap `wmill.yaml`.
* Variable manifests follow the schema documented in the https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable: the YAML must contain `value`, `is_secret: true`, and the other standard fields. Pushing with `--plain-secrets` lets you keep the value in clear text inside the manifest while still marking it as a secret in the workspace.
* Script assets live as file pairs on disk; see the https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python and the https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference for the exact `.script.yaml` shape and the `includeSecrets` toggle.
* The Windmill Python SDK exposes `wmill.get_variable(path: str)`; use it inside `main()` and print the result. Returning the value from `main()` will also be captured in the masked logs, so a plain `print` is sufficient.
* To execute the deployed script and obtain a job UUID you can either use `wmill script run` and capture the UUID it prints, or POST to `/api/w/<workspace>/jobs/run/p/<path>` with the bearer token and read the UUID from the response body.
* Logs are retrievable via `wmill job logs <uuid>` or via `GET /api/w/<workspace>/jobs_u/get_logs/<uuid>` if you want to confirm masking yourself.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Ensure the real Windmill deployment and execution actions are performed; the verifier will not redeploy anything.
* A `wmill.yaml` file exists at `/home/user/myproject/wmill.yaml`.
* A variable manifest exists at `/home/user/myproject/f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/api_key.variable.yaml` and is marked as a secret (`is_secret: true`).
* A Python script file pair exists at `/home/user/myproject/f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/print_secret.py` and `/home/user/myproject/f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/print_secret.script.yaml`, with the `.py` file invoking `wmill.get_variable("f/secret_masking_${run_id_safe}/api_key")`.
* Log file: `/home/user/myproject/output.log` contains exactly one line in the format `Job ID: <job_uuid>`, where `<job_uuid>` is the UUID of a completed run of the deployed script.
* Fetching the job logs through the Windmill API for that UUID must show that the secret value is masked: the logs must contain the prefix-and-stars form documented in the Windmill changelog (the first 3 characters of the secret followed by `*****`, i.e. `sec*****`) and must **not** contain the full plaintext value `secret_value_${run_id_safe}_padding`.
* The variable, folder, and script paths in the cloud workspace must all be suffixed with `${run_id_safe}` so concurrent runs do not collide.
I'll start by understanding the task and setting up the environment, then work through each step systematically.

content.text:
Title: Variables

URL Source: https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable

Markdown Content:
## Listing variables[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#listing-variables "Direct link to Listing variables")

The `wmill variable` list command is used to list all variables in the remote workspace.

`wmill variable`

## Adding a variable[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#adding-a-variable "Direct link to Adding a variable")

The `wmill variable add` command allows you to add a new variable to the remote workspace.

`wmill variable add <remote_path:string> --value=<value:string> [--secret] [--description=<description:string>] [--account=<account:number>] [--oauth]`

### Arguments[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#arguments "Direct link to Arguments")

| Argument | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `remote_path` | The path of the variable in the remote workspace. |

### Options[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#options "Direct link to Options")

| Option | parameter | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--value` | `<value>` | The value of the variable. |
| `--secret` | None | (Optional) Specifies whether the variable is a secret. |
| `--description` | `<description>` | (Optional) A description of the variable. |
| `--account` | `<account>` | (Optional) The account associated with the variable. |
| `--oauth` | None | (Optional) Specifies whether the variable requires OAuth authentication. |

### Example[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#example "Direct link to Example")

This command adds a new variable with the path "my_variable" to the remote workspace. The value is set to "12345", marked as a secret, and associated with account number 1. A description is also provided for the variable.

`wmill variable add my_variable --value=12345 --secret --description="My secret variable" --account=1`

## Pushing a variable[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#pushing-a-variable "Direct link to Pushing a variable")

The `wmill variable push` command allows you to push a local variable spec to the remote workspace, overriding any existing remote versions.

`wmill variable push <file_path:string> <remote_path:string> [--plain-secrets]`

### Arguments[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#arguments-1 "Direct link to Arguments")

| Argument | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `file_path` | The path to the variable file to push. |
| `remote_path` | The path of the variable in the remote workspace. |

### Options[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#options-1 "Direct link to Options")

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--plain-secrets` | (Optional) Specifies whether to push secrets as plain text. If provided, secrets will not be encrypted in the remote workspace. |

## Variable specification[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#variable-specification "Direct link to Variable specification")

### Structure[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#structure "Direct link to Structure")

Here is an example of a variable specification:

`{  value: string,  is_secret: boolean,  description: string,  extra_perms: object,  account: number,  is_oauth: boolean,  is_expired: boolean}`

### Example[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/variable#example-1 "Direct link to Example")

`{"value": "finland does not actually exist","is_secret": false,"description": "This item is not secret","extra_perms": {},"account": null,"is_oauth": false,"is_expired": false}`

content.text:
Title: wmill.yaml reference

URL Source: https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference

Markdown Content:
`wmill.yaml` is the configuration file for the Windmill CLI. It lives at the root of a project, binds workspaces to Windmill instances, and controls what [`wmill sync pull`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/sync#pull-api) and [`wmill sync push`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/sync#push-api) do by default. This page disects every option the CLI reads from it.

Auto-generated files

`wmill init` writes a fully commented starter file with every option documented inline. `wmill config` prints a table of every option with its type, default, and description (`wmill config --json` for a machine-readable version). A JSON Schema for editor autocompletion is committed at [`cli/wmill.schema.json`](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill/blob/main/cli/wmill.schema.json).

## File discovery[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#file-discovery "Direct link to File discovery")

When the CLI needs the config it walks up from the current directory looking for `wmill.yaml`, stopping at the git repository root (if any) or the filesystem root. If it finds the file in a parent directory, it logs a warning and changes the working directory there — so commands always run from the project root regardless of where they were invoked.

If no file is found, most commands print `No wmill.yaml found. Use 'wmill init' to bootstrap it.` and fall back to the built-in defaults listed below.

Add this header to get editor autocompletion and validation in most editors (VS Code, Zed, JetBrains) via the YAML Language Server:

`# yaml-language-server: $schema=wmill.schema.json`

## Starter example[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#starter-example "Direct link to Starter example")

A typical `wmill.yaml` for a single-workspace project:

`# yaml-language-server: $schema=wmill.schema.jsondefaultTs: bunincludes:  - "f/**"excludes: []skipSecrets: truenonDottedPaths: truesyncBehavior: v1workspaces:  main:    baseUrl: REDACTED    # workspaceId defaults to "main"    # gitBranch defaults to "main"`

## Options at a glance[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#options-at-a-glance "Direct link to Options at a glance")

| Option | Type | Default | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [`defaultTs`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#defaultts) | `"bun" | "deno"` | `bun` | TypeScript runtime for `.ts` scripts |
| [`includes`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includes) | `string[]` | `["f/**"]` | Glob patterns to include in sync |
| [`extraIncludes`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#extraincludes) | `string[]` | `[]` | Extra include patterns merged on top |
| [`excludes`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#excludes) | `string[]` | `[]` | Glob patterns to exclude from sync |
| [`skipVariables`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipvariables) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing variables |
| [`skipResources`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipresources) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing resources |
| [`skipResourceTypes`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipresourcetypes) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing resource types |
| [`skipSecrets`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipsecrets) | `boolean` | `true` | Skip syncing secret variables |
| [`skipScripts`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipscripts) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing scripts |
| [`skipFlows`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipflows) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing flows |
| [`skipApps`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipapps) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing apps |
| [`skipFolders`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipfolders) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip syncing folders |
| [`skipWorkspaceDependencies`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipworkspacedependencies) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip workspace dependencies |
| [`includeSchedules`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includeschedules) | `boolean` | `false` | Include schedules |
| [`includeTriggers`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includetriggers) | `boolean` | `false` | Include triggers (HTTP, WebSocket, Kafka, …) |
| [`includeUsers`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includeusers) | `boolean` | `false` | Include workspace users |
| [`includeGroups`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includegroups) | `boolean` | `false` | Include workspace groups |
| [`includeSettings`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includesettings) | `boolean` | `false` | Include workspace settings |
| [`includeKey`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includekey) | `boolean` | `false` | Include the workspace encryption key |
| [`parallel`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#parallel) | `integer` | unset (`1`) | Parallel operations during push |
| [`locksRequired`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#locksrequired) | `boolean` | `false` | Fail push if any script lacks a lock |
| [`lint`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#lint) | `boolean` | `false` | Run lint before push |
| [`plainSecrets`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#plainsecrets) | `boolean` | `false` | Store secrets in plain text |
| [`message`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#message) | `string` | unset | Default commit message for push |
| [`promotion`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#promotion) | `string` | unset | Branch whose `promotionOverrides` to apply |
| [`skipBranchValidation`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipbranchvalidation) | `boolean` | `false` | Skip git-branch ↔ workspace matching |
| [`nonDottedPaths`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#nondottedpaths) | `boolean` | `false` (`true` via `wmill init`) | Use `__flow`/`__app`/`__raw_app` suffixes |
| [`syncBehavior`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#syncbehavior) | `string` (`v1`) | `v1` | Ownership-handling behavior version |
| [`codebases`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#codebases) | `object[]` | `[]` | Bundled codebases shared across scripts |
| [`workspaces`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#workspaces) | `object` | `{}` | Workspace bindings and per-workspace overrides |
| [`gitBranches`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#gitbranches-deprecated) | `object` | — | **Deprecated** — use `workspaces` |
| [`environments`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#environments-deprecated) | `object` | — | **Deprecated** — use `workspaces` |
| [`git_branches`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#git_branches-deprecated) | `object` | — | **Deprecated** — use `workspaces` |
| [`overrides`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#overrides-removed) | `object` | — | **Removed** — errors on read |

## Core options[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#core-options "Direct link to Core options")

### `defaultTs`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#defaultts "Direct link to defaultts")

*   Type: `"bun" | "deno"`
*   Default: `bun`

Runtime used when a `.ts` file's language cannot be inferred from its filename (`*.bun.ts` and `*.deno.ts` override this). Also used by [`wmill script bootstrap`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/script) and by lock/metadata generation to pick the right dependency resolver.

`defaultTs: bun   # or: deno`

### `includes`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includes "Direct link to includes")

*   Type: `string[]`
*   Default: `["f/**"]`

Glob patterns limiting which local files participate in sync. Matches both pulled and pushed items. Standard glob syntax applies: `*` matches within a path segment, `**` matches across segments.

Overridden entirely when the `-i, --includes` CLI flag is passed.

`includes:  - "f/**"        # everything under the f/ root  - "u/alex/**"   # one user's personal namespace`

*   Type: `string[]`
*   Default: `[]`

Additional patterns merged with `includes` (both must match for a file to be considered). Useful inside a workspace `overrides:` block to tighten the scope for one workspace without rewriting the base `includes` list. Mirrors the `--extra-includes` CLI flag.

### `excludes`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#excludes "Direct link to excludes")

*   Type: `string[]`
*   Default: `[]`

Glob patterns of files to exclude from sync, applied after `includes`. Overridden entirely by the `-e, --excludes` CLI flag.

## What to sync[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#what-to-sync "Direct link to What to sync")

The CLI syncs a core set of items by default (scripts, flows, apps, resources, resource types, variables, folders) and opts out of the rest (secrets, schedules, triggers, users, groups, settings, encryption key). The `skip*` flags remove items from the default set; the `include*` flags opt additional categories in.

### `skipVariables`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipvariables "Direct link to skipvariables")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips all variables (including any secret variables) on both pull and push. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-variables`.

### `skipResources`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipresources "Direct link to skipresources")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips resources. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-resources`.

### `skipResourceTypes`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipresourcetypes "Direct link to skipresourcetypes")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips resource type definitions. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-resource-types`.

### `skipSecrets`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipsecrets "Direct link to skipsecrets")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `true`

Skips _only_ secret variables (non-secret variables still sync unless `skipVariables` is also set). Defaults to `true` so secret values never leave the Windmill workspace unintentionally. Set to `false` only when you have understood the security implications. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-secrets`; `--include-secrets` overrides this setting from the CLI.

### `skipScripts`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipscripts "Direct link to skipscripts")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips scripts. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-scripts`.

### `skipFlows`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipflows "Direct link to skipflows")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips flows. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-flows`.

### `skipApps`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipapps "Direct link to skipapps")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips [apps](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/apps/app_editor). Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-apps`.

### `skipFolders`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipfolders "Direct link to skipfolders")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips [folders](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/groups_and_folders) (their metadata and ACLs). Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-folders`.

### `skipWorkspaceDependencies`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipworkspacedependencies "Direct link to skipworkspacedependencies")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Skips the workspace-level shared dependency manifests (`package.json`, `requirements.txt`, …) used by TypeScript/Python scripts. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-workspace-dependencies`.

### `includeSchedules`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includeschedules "Direct link to includeschedules")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Includes [schedules](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/scheduling) in the sync scope. Equivalent CLI flag: `--include-schedules`.

### `includeTriggers`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includetriggers "Direct link to includetriggers")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Includes all [triggers](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/triggers) (HTTP routes, WebSocket, Postgres, Kafka, NATS, SQS, GCP Pub/Sub, MQTT). Equivalent CLI flag: `--include-triggers`.

### `includeUsers`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includeusers "Direct link to includeusers")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Includes workspace user memberships. Equivalent CLI flag: `--include-users`.

### `includeGroups`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includegroups "Direct link to includegroups")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Includes [groups](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/groups_and_folders) and their memberships. Equivalent CLI flag: `--include-groups`.

### `includeSettings`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includesettings "Direct link to includesettings")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Includes workspace-level settings (default timezone, auto-invite, error handler, …). Equivalent CLI flag: `--include-settings`.

### `includeKey`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#includekey "Direct link to includekey")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Includes the workspace encryption key. Only relevant when cloning a workspace to a new instance and you want secrets to decrypt without re-encryption. Equivalent CLI flag: `--include-key`.

## Sync behavior[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#sync-behavior "Direct link to Sync behavior")

### `parallel`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#parallel "Direct link to parallel")

*   Type: `integer`
*   Default: unset (runs sequentially with concurrency `1`)

Number of changes applied in parallel during push. Values `<= 0` are clamped to `1`. Folder-metadata changes are always applied sequentially first (so ACLs are in place before items reference them), then the rest of the changes run through the parallel pool. Equivalent CLI flag: `--parallel <n>`.

### `locksRequired`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#locksrequired "Direct link to locksrequired")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

When `true`, `wmill sync push` fails upfront if any script (or flow inline script) that should have a lockfile is missing one. Prevents accidentally pushing non-reproducible scripts. Equivalent CLI flag: `--locks-required`.

### `lint`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#lint "Direct link to lint")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Runs `wmill lint` before push. If any errors are reported, the push aborts. Equivalent CLI flag: `--lint`. See [Lint](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/lint).

### `plainSecrets`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#plainsecrets "Direct link to plainsecrets")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

When pulling, stores secret variable values in clear text in local YAML instead of the default encrypted form. When pushing, treats local secret values as already in clear text. Not recommended — use only in development or when secrets are already managed by an external secret store. Equivalent CLI flag: `--plain-secrets`.

### `message`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#message "Direct link to message")

*   Type: `string`
*   Default: unset

Default commit-like message attached to every script, flow, and app that sync push updates in this run. Overridden by `--message <string>`.

### `promotion`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#promotion "Direct link to promotion")

*   Type: `string`
*   Default: unset

Name of the _source_ workspace whose [`promotionOverrides`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#workspaces) to apply on top of the current workspace during push. Typical use: deploying code tested on `staging` to `prod`. Mirrors the `--promotion <name>` CLI flag and is typically set per invocation rather than in the file.

### `skipBranchValidation`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#skipbranchvalidation "Direct link to skipbranchvalidation")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false`

Disables the safety check that requires the current git branch to match an entry in `workspaces:`. Handy for throwaway feature branches. Equivalent CLI flag: `--skip-branch-validation`.

### `nonDottedPaths`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#nondottedpaths "Direct link to nondottedpaths")

*   Type: `boolean`
*   Default: `false` (set to `true` by `wmill init` for new projects)

Controls how flow/app/raw-app directories are named on disk.

*   `true` → `my_flow__flow/`, `my_app__app/`, `my_raw_app__raw_app/`
*   `false` → `my_flow.flow/`, `my_app.app/`, `my_raw_app.raw_app/`

The non-dotted form avoids edge cases on Windows and with tooling that treats `.flow` as a file extension. Recommended for new projects.

### `syncBehavior`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#syncbehavior "Direct link to syncbehavior")

*   Type: `string` (format: `vN`)
*   Default: `v1`

Version knob for sync semantics. `v1` enables:

*   **Ownership preservation on push**: updating an existing item keeps its `permissioned_as` owner instead of silently rewriting it to the caller.
*   **`on_behalf_of_email` stripped on pull**: the transient "acting user" field is never written to disk.

If not set, the CLI warns that ownership preservation is disabled. If set to a version newer than the CLI supports, the CLI errors and suggests `wmill upgrade`.

`syncBehavior: v1`

## Codebase bundling[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#codebase-bundling "Direct link to Codebase bundling")

### `codebases`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#codebases "Direct link to codebases")

*   Type: `object[]`
*   Default: `[]`

List of shared TypeScript/JavaScript projects bundled and uploaded alongside scripts so multiple scripts can import from a common library. Each entry runs through `esbuild` (or a custom bundler) during push. See [Sharing common logic](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/sharing_common_logic) and [Codebases and bundles](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/codebases_and_bundles) for background.

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `relative_path` | `string`_(required)_ | Path to the codebase directory (relative to `wmill.yaml`) |
| `includes` | `string[]` | Glob patterns of files to bundle |
| `excludes` | `string[]` | Glob patterns of files to exclude |
| `format` | `"cjs" | "esm"` | Output module format |
| `external` | `string[]` | Packages left unbundled at runtime |
| `assets` | `{ from: string; to: string }[]` | Static files copied into the bundle |
| `customBundler` | `string` | Path to a custom bundler script (replaces esbuild) |
| `inject` | `string[]` | Files injected into every entry point |
| `define` | `Record<string, string>` | Compile-time constants |
| `banner` | `Record<string, string>` | Text prepended to output files by type (`js`, `css`, …) |
| `loader` | `Record<string, string>` | esbuild loader overrides by file extension |

`codebases:  - relative_path: ./shared    includes: ["**/*.ts"]    excludes: ["**/*.test.ts", "node_modules/**"]    format: esm    external: ["pg", "axios"]    assets:      - from: ./static        to: ./dist    define:      API_URL: '"https://api.example.com"'    inject: ["./polyfills.ts"]    banner:      js: "/* bundled by windmill */"    loader:      ".png": "dataurl"    # customBundler: ./build.ts`

## Workspaces[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#workspaces "Direct link to Workspaces")

### `workspaces`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#workspaces-1 "Direct link to workspaces-1")

*   Type: `object`
*   Default: `{}`

Map from a human-friendly workspace name to a Windmill instance and per-workspace sync configuration. The map key doubles as the default workspace id (`workspaceId`), the default git branch (`gitBranch`), and the filename suffix for [workspace-specific items](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/environment-specific-items).

`workspaces:  dev:    baseUrl: https://dev.windmill.dev    overrides:      skipSecrets: false  prod:    baseUrl: REDACTED    workspaceId: prod-ws     # set only if it differs from the key    gitBranch: main          # set only if it differs from the key    overrides:      skipSecrets: false      includeSchedules: true    promotionOverrides:      skipApps: true    specificItems:      resources: ["u/alex/prod_*"]      variables: ["u/alex/env_*"]  commonSpecificItems:    resources: ["u/alex/config/**"]    folders: ["f/env_*"]    settings: true`

The key `commonSpecificItems` is reserved — it is not a workspace, it declares items that should be stored per-workspace for _every_ workspace.

#### Per-workspace fields[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#per-workspace-fields "Direct link to Per-workspace fields")

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `baseUrl` | `string` | Windmill instance URL (e.g. `REDACTED`). The CLI matches this against local [workspace profiles](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/workspace-management) so the same file works on any machine logged into the instance. |
| `workspaceId` | `string` | Windmill workspace id. Defaults to the map key. |
| `gitBranch` | `string` | Git branch name this workspace is auto-selected on. Defaults to the map key. |
| `overrides` | `Partial<SyncOptions>` | Any top-level sync option, overridden when this workspace is active. |
| `promotionOverrides` | `Partial<SyncOptions>` | Overrides applied when this workspace is the _source_ of a `--promotion` push. Falls back to `overrides` if unset. |
| `specificItems` | `object` | Items stored per-workspace on disk (see below). |

#### `specificItems` and `commonSpecificItems`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#specificitems-and-commonspecificitems "Direct link to specificitems-and-commonspecificitems")

Both share the same shape. Items whose path matches one of these patterns get the workspace name inserted into the local filename (`database.resource.yaml` ↔ `database.prod.resource.yaml` on disk). Full details in [Workspace-specific items](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/environment-specific-items).

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `variables` | `string[]` | Variable path patterns |
| `resources` | `string[]` | Resource path patterns |
| `triggers` | `string[]` | Trigger path patterns |
| `folders` | `string[]` | Folder path patterns |
| `settings` | `boolean` | Treat `settings.yaml` as per-workspace |

`schedules` pattern list

The internal `SpecificItemsConfig_Yaml` type also accepts a `schedules` key, but it is currently not part of the JSON Schema and is reserved for future use. Prefer `triggers` for now.

### Workspace resolution order[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#workspace-resolution-order "Direct link to Workspace resolution order")

1.   `--workspace <name>` — explicit CLI override.
2.   **Current git branch** — the first entry whose effective `gitBranch` (the `gitBranch` field, or the map key if unset) matches `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`.
3.   **Local profile** — the workspace selected via [`wmill workspace switch`](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/workspace-management).

### Settings resolution priority[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#settings-resolution-priority "Direct link to Settings resolution priority")

When multiple layers define the same option, the highest-priority one wins:

1.   CLI flags (highest).
2.   `promotionOverrides` of the workspace pointed to by `--promotion`.
3.   `overrides` of the resolved workspace.
4.   Top-level options in `wmill.yaml`.
5.   Built-in defaults (lowest).

## Deprecated and removed keys[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#deprecated-and-removed-keys "Direct link to Deprecated and removed keys")

The CLI reads the legacy keys below and normalizes them to `workspaces` in memory, emitting a one-time deprecation warning. Run `wmill config migrate` to rewrite the file in the new format — it preserves every field (`baseUrl`, `workspaceId`, `overrides`, `promotionOverrides`, `specificItems`, `commonSpecificItems`).

### `gitBranches`_(deprecated)_[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#gitbranches-deprecated "Direct link to gitbranches-deprecated")

Legacy multi-branch map. Keys were git branch names (not freeform workspace names). Has the same per-entry shape as `workspaces`. Priority over `environments` and `git_branches` if multiple legacy keys are present.

### `environments`_(deprecated)_[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#environments-deprecated "Direct link to environments-deprecated")

Legacy single-branch alias of `gitBranches` intended for promotion workflows on one branch. Same per-entry shape. Only read if `workspaces` and `gitBranches` are both absent.

### `git_branches`_(deprecated)_[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#git_branches-deprecated "Direct link to git_branches-deprecated")

Snake-case variant of `gitBranches`, kept for historical configs. Lowest priority among the legacy keys.

### `overrides`_(removed)_[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#overrides-removed "Direct link to overrides-removed")

Was previously a top-level key holding per-branch overrides. Reading a non-empty `overrides` now errors:

`❌ The 'overrides' field is no longer supported.   The configuration system now uses workspace-based configuration.   Please delete your wmill.yaml and run 'wmill init' to recreate it with the new format.`

Move the settings into the matching entry under `workspaces:` (`overrides:` lives _inside_ a workspace entry, not at the top level).

## Fields not meant for `wmill.yaml`[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#fields-not-meant-for-wmillyaml "Direct link to fields-not-meant-for-wmillyaml")

The internal `SyncOptions` type also contains fields backing one-off CLI flags (`yes`, `dryRun`, `skipPull`, `failConflicts`, `json`, `jsonOutput`, `stateful`, `raw`). Setting these in `wmill.yaml` is technically possible but discouraged — they describe _one invocation_, not a project's baseline. In particular:

*   `raw` is now the default on push. The CLI prints `--raw is now the default, you can remove it as a flag` if set.
*   `stateful` enables `.wmill/` state tracking. The file-based alternative (sync from a clean checkout every time) is recommended instead.

## See also[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli/wmill-yaml-reference#see-also "Direct link to See also")

content.text:
Title: Python quickstart

URL Source: https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python

Markdown Content:
In this quick start guide, we will write our first script in Python.

[Video 9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QRf8C8qF7CY)
This tutorial covers how to create a simple script through Windmill web IDE. See the dedicated page to [develop scripts locally](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/local_development) and other methods of [handling dependencies in Python](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/dependencies_in_python).

Scripts are the basic building blocks in Windmill. They can be [run and scheduled](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/triggers) as standalone, chained together to create [Flows](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_editor) or displayed with a personalized User Interface as [Apps](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/apps_quickstart).

Scripts consist of 2 parts:

*   [Code](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#code): for Python scripts, it must have at least a main function.
*   [Settings](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#settings): settings & metadata about the Script such as its path, summary, description, [JSON Schema](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/json_schema_and_parsing) of its inputs (inferred from its signature).

When stored in a code repository, these 2 parts are stored separately at `<path>.py` and `<path>.script.yaml`

Windmill automatically manages [dependencies](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/dependencies_in_python) for you. When you import libraries in your Python script, Windmill parses these top-level imports upon saving the script and automatically generates a list of dependencies. For automatic dependency installation, Windmill will only consider these top-level imports. It then spawns a dependency job to associate these PyPI packages with a lockfile, ensuring that the same version of the script is always executed with the same versions of its dependencies

[This](https://hub.windmill.dev/scripts/%22%22/1530/do-sentiment-analysis-with-nltk-%22%22) is a simple example of a script built in Python with Windmill:

`#import wmillimport nltkfrom nltk.sentiment import SentimentIntensityAnalyzernltk.download("vader_lexicon")def main(text: str = "Wow, NLTK is really powerful!"):    return SentimentIntensityAnalyzer().polarity_scores(text)`

In this quick start guide, we'll create a script that greets the operator running it.

From the Home page, click `+Script`. This will take you to the first step of script creation: [Metadata](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/script_editor/settings#metadata).

## Settings[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#settings "Direct link to Settings")

![Image 1: New script](https://www.windmill.dev/assets/images/script_languages-39a8a865504999a017a88960c3fc9a2a.png)

As part of the [settings](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/script_editor/settings) menu, each script has metadata associated with it, enabling it to be defined and configured in depth.

*   **Summary** (optional) is a short, human-readable summary of the Script. It will be displayed as a title across Windmill. If omitted, the UI will use the `path` by default.
*   **Path** is the Script's unique identifier that consists of the [script's owner](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/roles_and_permissions), and the script's name. The owner can be either a user, or a group ([folder](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/groups_and_folders#folders)).
*   **Description** is where you can give instructions through the [auto-generated UI](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/auto_generated_uis) to users on how to run your Script. It supports markdown.
*   **Language** of the script.
*   **Script kind**: Action (by default), [Trigger](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_trigger), [Approval](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_approval), [Error handler](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_error_handler) or [Preprocessor](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/preprocessors). This acts as a tag to filter appropriate scripts from the [flow editor](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/flows_quickstart).

This menu also has additional settings on [Runtime](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/script_editor/settings#runtime), [Generated UI](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#generated-ui) and [Triggers](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/script_editor/settings#triggers).

Now click on the code editor on the left side, and let's build our Hello World!

## Code[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#code "Direct link to Code")

Windmill provides an online editor to work on your Scripts. The left-side is the editor itself. The right-side [previews the UI](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/auto_generated_uis) that Windmill will generate from the Script's signature - this will be visible to the users of the Script. You can preview that UI, provide input values, and [test your script](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#instant-preview--testing) there.

![Image 2: Editor for python](https://www.windmill.dev/assets/images/editor_python.png-e81ff5457ecbfe7879ca64764ba966b3.webp)

As we picked `python` for this example, Windmill provided some python boilerplate. Let's take a look:

`import osimport wmill# You can import any PyPI package. # See here for more info: https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/dependencies_in_python# you can use typed resources by doing a type alias to dict#postgresql = dictdef main(    no_default: str,    #db: postgresql,    name="Nicolas Bourbaki",    age=42,    obj: dict = {"even": "dicts"},    l: list = ["or", "lists!"],    file_: bytes = bytes(0),):    print(f"Hello World and a warm welcome especially to {name}")    print("and its acolytes..", age, obj, l, len(file_))    # retrieve variables, resources, states using the wmill client    try:        secret = wmill.get_variable("f/examples/secret")    except:        secret = "No secret yet at f/examples/secret !"    print(f"The variable at `f/examples/secret`: {secret}")    # Get last state of this script execution by the same trigger/user    last_state = wmill.get_state()    new_state = {"foo": 42} if last_state is None else last_state    new_state["foo"] += 1    wmill.set_state(new_state)    # fetch context variables    user = os.environ.get("WM_USERNAME")    # return value is converted to JSON    return {"splitted": name.split(), "user": user, "state": new_state}`

In Windmill, scripts need to have a `main` function that will be the script's entrypoint. There are a few important things to note about the `main`.

*   The main arguments are used for generating 
    1.   the [input spec](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/json_schema_and_parsing) of the Script
    2.   the [frontend](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/auto_generated_uis) that you see when running the Script as a standalone app.

*   Type annotations are used to generate the UI form, and help pre-validate inputs. While not mandatory, they are highly recommended. You can customize the UI in later steps (but not change the input type!).
*   Windmill supports [Pydantic `BaseModel`](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) and [`@dataclass`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html) classes as parameter types. When a parameter is typed with a Pydantic model or dataclass, Windmill infers the JSON schema from the class fields and generates a structured input form. Nested models, `Optional`, `List`, and `Dict` types are supported.

### Pydantic and dataclass support[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#pydantic-and-dataclass-support "Direct link to Pydantic and dataclass support")

You can use Pydantic `BaseModel` or Python `@dataclass` as parameter types for your main function. Windmill will parse the class definition and generate a structured input form with proper field types.

`from pydantic import BaseModelfrom typing import Optional, Listclass Address(BaseModel):    street: str    city: str    zip_code: Optional[str] = Noneclass User(BaseModel):    name: str    age: int    addresses: List[Address] = []def main(user: User):    return f"Hello {user.name}, age {user.age}"`

Dataclasses work the same way:

`from dataclasses import dataclass@dataclassclass Config:    host: str    port: int = 8080    debug: bool = Falsedef main(config: Config):    return f"Connecting to {config.host}:{config.port}"`

The last import line imports the [Windmill client](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/clients/python_client), which is needed for example to access [variables](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/variables_and_secrets) or [resources](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/resources_and_types).

Back to our Hello World. We can clean up unused import statements, change the main to take in the user's name. Let's also return the `name`, maybe we can use this later if we use this Script within a [flow](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_editor) or [app](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/apps/app_editor) and need to pass its result on.

`def main(name: str):  print("Hello world. Oh, it's you {}? Greetings!".format(name))  return name`

## Instant preview & testing[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#instant-preview--testing "Direct link to Instant preview & testing")

Look at the UI preview on the right: it was updated to match the input signature. Run a test (`Ctrl` + `Enter`) to verify everything works.

[Video 10](https://www.windmill.dev/videos/auto_g_ui_landing.mp4)
You can change how the UI behaves by changing the main signature. For example, if you add a default for the `name` argument, the UI won't consider this field as required anymore.

`def main(name: str = "you"):`

Now let's go to the last step: the "Generated UI" settings.

## Generated UI[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#generated-ui "Direct link to Generated UI")

From the Settings menu, the "Generated UI" tab lets you customize the script's arguments.

The UI is generated from the Script's main function signature, but you can add additional constraints here. For example, we could use the `Customize property`: add a regex by clicking on `Pattern` to make sure users are providing a name with only alphanumeric characters: `^[A-Za-z0-9]+$`. Let's still allow numbers in case you are some tech billionaire's kid.

![Image 3: Advanced settings for Python](https://www.windmill.dev/assets/images/customize_python.png-77977a5c411cd9787b6841ccdb4d474f.webp)

## Workflows as code[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#workflows-as-code "Direct link to Workflows as code")

One way to write distributed programs that execute distinct jobs is to use [flows](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_editor) that chain scripts together.

Another approach is to write a program that defines the jobs and their dependencies, and then execute that program directly in your script. This is known as [workflows as code](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/workflows_as_code). Use the `@workflow` decorator on your orchestration function and `@task` on task functions. Each task runs as a separate job with its own logs and timeline entry, while the workflow suspends between tasks (releasing its worker slot).

![Image 4: Flow as code in Python](https://www.windmill.dev/assets/images/wac-editor-1-c3756514ee2aeea7be9bbb30820bd7ef.png)

All details at:

## Run![​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#run "Direct link to Run!")

We're done! Now let's look at what users of the script will do. Click on the [Deploy](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/draft_and_deploy) button to load the script. You'll see the user input form we defined earlier.

Note that Scripts are [versioned](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/versioning#script-versioning) in Windmill, and each script version is uniquely identified by a hash.

Fill in the input field, then hit "Run". You should see a run view, as well as your logs. All script runs are also available in the [Runs](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/monitor_past_and_future_runs) menu on the left.

![Image 5: Run hello world in Python](https://www.windmill.dev/assets/images/run_python.png-1139d41ea9d6e52614fd78f208919d3c.webp)

You can also choose to [run the script from the CLI](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/advanced/cli) with the pre-made Command-line interface call.

## Select Python version[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#select-python-version "Direct link to Select Python version")

You can annotate the version of Python you would like to use for a script using the following annotations: py310, py311, py312, or py313:

`# py312type Foo = strdef main():	foo: Foo = "Foo"	return foo`

### Python version specifiers[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#python-version-specifiers "Direct link to Python version specifiers")

You can also use more advanced version specifiers with the shortcut format to specify exact version requirements:

`# py: >=3.12def main():    return "Hello from Python 3.12+"`

`# py: ==3.12.*def main():    return "Hello from any Python 3.12 version"`

`# py: >=3.11,<3.14def main():    return "Hello from Python 3.11 to 3.13"`

These version specifiers use [PEP 440](https://peps.python.org/pep-0440/) syntax and provide more precise control over Python version requirements than the simple annotations. This is especially useful when you need to ensure compatibility with specific Python features or avoid known issues in certain versions.

[Video 11](https://www.windmill.dev/videos/annotate_py_version.mp4)
Alternatively, you can set a global version by configuring the INSTANCE_PYTHON_VERSION [environment variable](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/environment_variables) to one of the mentioned versions or unset it to use "Latest Stable". If you leave `INSTANCE_PYTHON_VERSION` empty it will inherit "Latest Stable" version, which depends on Windmill.

[Video 12](https://www.windmill.dev/videos/set_instance_py_version.mp4)
For newly deployed scripts, the annotated or instance version will be assigned to the lockfile, and all future executions will adhere to that specified version.

For scripts that are already deployed and have no version specified in lockfile, Python 3.11 will be used by default, even if the instance version is changed to a different one.

During test runs or deployments, if there are imported scripts, Windmill will search through all of them to find an annotated version, which will be used as the final version. If no annotated version is found, the instance version will be used instead.

For [Enterprise Edition](https://www.windmill.dev/pricing) (EE) customers, [S3 cache](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/misc/s3_cache) tarballs will be organized and separated by Python version.

## Caching[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#caching "Direct link to Caching")

Every dependency on Python is cached on disk by default. Furtherfore if you use the [Distributed cache storage](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/misc/s3_cache), it will be available to every other worker, allowing fast startup for every worker.

## What's next?[​](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/scripts_quickstart/python#whats-next "Direct link to What's next?")

This script is a minimal working example, but there's a few more steps that can be useful in a real-world use case:

*   Pass [variables and secrets](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/variables_and_secrets) to a script.
*   Connect to [resources](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/resources_and_types).
*   [Trigger that script](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/triggers) in many ways.
*   Compose scripts in [Flows](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/flows/flow_editor), [low-code apps](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/apps/app_editor) or [full-code apps](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/full_code_apps).
*   You can [share your scripts](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/misc/share_on_hub) with the community on [Windmill Hub](https://hub.windmill.dev/). Once submitted, they will be verified by moderators before becoming available to everyone right within Windmill.

Scripts are immutable and there is an hash for each deployment of a given script. Scripts are never overwritten and referring to a script by path is referring to the latest deployed hash at that path.

For each script, a UI is autogenerated from the jsonchema inferred from the script signature, and can be customized further as standalone or embedded into rich UIs using the [App builder](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/getting_started/apps_quickstart).

In addition to the UI, sync and async [webhooks](https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/webhooks) are generated for each deployment.

content.text:
Title: Script Structure and Schema

URL Source: https://deepwiki.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/5.1-script-structure-and-schema

Published Time: 2025-10-17T00:12:46.183090

Markdown Content:
Relevant source files
*   [f/delivery/cleared_script.deno.ts](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.deno.ts)
*   [f/delivery/cleared_script.script.lock](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.lock)
*   [f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml)
*   [f/delivery/folder.meta.yaml](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/folder.meta.yaml)
*   [f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock)
*   [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml)
*   [f/examples/snappy_script.ts](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts)

## Purpose and Scope

This document explains the structure of Windmill scripts in the repository, focusing on the three-file pattern used to define each script and how JSON Schema definitions in YAML files map to TypeScript implementations. This page covers the schema definition format, property types, and the relationship between configuration files and executable code.

For information about implementing the TypeScript code itself, see [TypeScript Implementation](https://deepwiki.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/5.2-typescript-implementation). For runtime selection between Bun and Deno, see [Runtime Selection: Bun vs Deno](https://deepwiki.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/5.3-runtime-selection:-bun-vs-deno). For complete working examples, see [Example: snappy_script](https://deepwiki.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/5.4-example:-snappy_script) and [Example: cleared_script](https://deepwiki.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/5.5-example:-cleared_script).

## Three-File Pattern

Each Windmill script consists of three related files that work together:

| File Type | Extension | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Schema Definition | `.script.yaml` | Defines metadata, JSON Schema for parameters, and references the lock file |
| Implementation | `.ts` or `.deno.ts` | Contains the executable TypeScript code with the `main()` function |
| Dependency Lock | `.script.lock` | Stores resolved dependencies and their versions |

**Title: Script File Components and Relationships**

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 1-38](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L1-L38)[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 1-19](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L1-L19)[f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock 1-5](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock#L1-L5)

### File Naming Convention

The three files share a common base name with different extensions:

*   `{script_name}.script.yaml`
*   `{script_name}.ts` (for Bun runtime) or `{script_name}.deno.ts` (for Deno runtime)
*   `{script_name}.script.lock`

For example, the `snappy_script` consists of:

*   [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 1-38](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L1-L38)
*   [f/examples/snappy_script.ts 1-19](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L1-L19)
*   [f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock 1-5](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock#L1-L5)

## YAML Schema Definition Structure

The `.script.yaml` file defines the script's interface using JSON Schema draft 2020-12 format. The root structure contains:

| Field | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `summary` | Brief description of the script | Empty string in examples |
| `description` | Detailed description | Empty string in examples |
| `lock` | Reference to the lock file | `!inline f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock` |
| `kind` | Resource type identifier | `script` |
| `schema` | JSON Schema object defining parameters | See below |

The `lock` field uses the `!inline` YAML tag to reference the lock file path: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 3](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L3-L3)

### Schema Object Structure

The `schema` object follows JSON Schema draft 2020-12 specification [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 6](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L6-L6):

`schema:  $schema: 'https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema'  type: object  properties:    # Parameter definitions  required:    # Required parameter names`
Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 5-37](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L5-L37)[f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml 5-37](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml#L5-L37)

## Schema Property Types and Mappings

Each parameter in the script's `main()` function is defined as a property in the schema. The schema supports multiple property types that map to TypeScript types.

**Title: Schema Property Type Mappings**

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 8-36](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L8-L36)[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 7-14](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L7-L14)

### Number Type

Defines numeric parameters:

`a:  type: number  description: ''  default: null`
Maps to TypeScript: `a: number`[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 8](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L8-L8)

When a number property is listed in the `required` array, it must be provided by the caller and cannot have a default value in TypeScript.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 9-12](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L9-L12)

### String Type with Enum

Defines string parameters with a constrained set of allowed values:

`b:  type: string  description: ''  default: null  enum:    - my    - ennn  originalType: enum`
Maps to TypeScript union type: `b: "my" | "ennn"`[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 9](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L9-L9)

The `originalType: enum` field indicates this property represents an enumeration. The `enum` array lists all allowed values.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 13-20](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L13-L20)[f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml 14-20](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml#L14-L20)

### String Type with Default

Defines string parameters with default values:

`e:  type: string  description: ''  default: inferred type string from default arg  originalType: string`
Maps to TypeScript with default parameter: `e = "inferred type string from default arg"`[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 12](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L12-L12)

When a `default` value is provided in the schema, the TypeScript parameter uses that value as its default, making the parameter optional for callers.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 21-25](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L21-L25)

### Object Type with Nested Properties

Defines object parameters with structured nested fields:

`f:  type: object  description: ''  default:    nested: object  properties:    nested:      type: string      originalType: string`
Maps to TypeScript with default object: `f = { nested: "object" }`[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 13](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L13-L13)

The `properties` field defines the schema for nested object fields. Each nested property can have its own type specification using `originalType`.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 26-34](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L26-L34)

### Required Parameters

The `required` array at the schema root lists parameter names that must be provided:

`required:  - a  - b`
Required parameters do not have defaults in TypeScript and must be provided by the caller. In the examples, both `a` (number) and `b` (enum) are required, while `e` (string with default) and `f` (object with default) are optional.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 35-37](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L35-L37)[f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml 35-37](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml#L35-L37)

## Lock File Format

The `.script.lock` file stores dependency information in a runtime-specific format.

### Bun Lock Format

For scripts using the Bun runtime (`.ts` extension), the lock file contains a JSON dependencies object followed by a binary lockfile reference:

`{  "dependencies": {}}//bun.lockb<empty>`
The `//bun.lockb` comment indicates that Bun's binary lock format is stored separately. In this example, there are no dependencies, so the binary lockfile is empty.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock 1-5](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.lock#L1-L5)

### Deno Lock Format

For scripts using the Deno runtime (`.deno.ts` extension), the lock file uses Deno's lock format version 3:

`{  "version": "3",  "remote": {}}`
The `remote` object would contain hashes of remote dependencies if any were imported. In this example, the script has no dependencies.

Sources: [f/delivery/cleared_script.script.lock 1-4](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.lock#L1-L4)

## Schema-to-Implementation Mapping

The complete mapping from YAML schema to TypeScript implementation follows this pattern:

**Title: Complete Schema to TypeScript Mapping**

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 5-37](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L5-L37)[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 7-16](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L7-L16)

### Mapping Rules

| Schema Characteristic | TypeScript Result |
| --- | --- |
| Property in `required` array + `type: number` | `param: number` (required parameter) |
| Property in `required` array + `enum` values | `param: "val1" | "val2"` (required union) |
| Property with `default` value | `param = defaultValue` (optional parameter) |
| `type: object` with `properties` | Nested object type inferred from default |
| `type: string` with `originalType: string` | `string` type |
| `type: string` with `originalType: enum` | Union type of enum values |

The schema serves as the contract between Windmill's UI and the script implementation. When a user runs the script through Windmill's interface, the UI renders form fields based on the schema properties, validates input against the schema, and passes validated parameters to the TypeScript `main()` function.

Sources: [f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml 1-38](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.script.yaml#L1-L38)[f/examples/snappy_script.ts 7-14](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/examples/snappy_script.ts#L7-L14)[f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml 1-38](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.script.yaml#L1-L38)[f/delivery/cleared_script.deno.ts 9-15](https://github.com/windmill-labs/windmill-sync-example/blob/6a6c4e9e/f/delivery/cleared_script.deno.ts#L9-L15)
